s19 (s19_mcp_plugin/code.py) -> s20 (s20_comprehensive/code.py)
11#!/usr/bin/env python3
22"""
3-s19: MCP Tools — MCPClient + tool discovery + assemble_tool_pool.
3+s20: Comprehensive Agent — all teaching components in one loop.
44
5-Run: python s19_mcp_plugin/code.py
6-Need: pip install anthropic python-dotenv + .env with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
5+Run: python s20_comprehensive/code.py
6+Need: pip install anthropic python-dotenv pyyaml + .env with ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
77
8-Changes from s18:
9- - MCPClient class: discovers tools, calls tools via mock handler
10- - normalize_mcp_name: normalize tool/server names
11- - assemble_tool_pool: assembles builtin + MCP tools into one pool
12- - connect_mcp: connect to an MCP server, discover tools
13- - Tool naming: mcp__{server}__{tool} with normalization
14- - MCP tools have readOnly/destructive annotations
15- - agent_loop uses dynamic tool pool (builtin + MCP), no prompt cache
16- - Teammate tools: complete_task, worktree cwd (from s17/s18 fixes)
17-
18-ASCII flow:
19- connect_mcp("docs") → MCPClient discovers tools →
20- assemble_tool_pool → [builtin... , mcp__docs__search, mcp__docs__get_version]
21- agent_loop uses assembled pool
8+This final chapter intentionally puts the earlier teaching mechanisms back
9+together: dispatch, permission, hooks, todo, subagent, skills, compaction,
10+memory, prompt assembly, error recovery, task graph, background tasks, cron,
11+teams, protocols, autonomous agents, worktrees, and MCP.
2212"""
2313

44+DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS = 8000
45+ESCALATED_MAX_TOKENS = 16000
46+MAX_RETRIES = 3
47+MAX_CONSECUTIVE_529 = 2
48+MAX_RECOVERY_RETRIES = 2
49+BASE_DELAY_MS = 500

51+KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_RESULTS = 3

53+CONTINUATION_PROMPT = "Continue from the previous response. Do not repeat completed work."
54+PROMPT = "\033[36ms20 >> \033[0m"
55+CLI_ACTIVE = False
56+
57+
58+def terminal_print(text: str):
59+ if threading.current_thread() is threading.main_thread() or not CLI_ACTIVE:
60+ print(text)
61+ return
62+ line = ""
63+ if READLINE_AVAILABLE:
64+ try:
65+ line = readline.get_line_buffer()
66+ except Exception:
67+ line = ""
68+ print(f"\r\033[K{text}")
69+ print(PROMPT + line, end="", flush=True)
70+
4671# ── Task System ──
4772
73+# Tasks are tiny durable records. Later systems add ownership, dependencies,
74+# worktrees, and teammates on top of this same file-backed state.
4875TASKS_DIR = WORKDIR / ".tasks"
4976TASKS_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
77+CURRENT_TODOS: list[dict] = []
5078
5179
5280@dataclass
5381class Task:
5482 id: str
5583 subject: str
5684 description: str
5785 status: str
5886 owner: str | None
5987 blockedBy: list[str]
6088 worktree: str | None = None
6189
6290
6391def _task_path(task_id: str) -> Path:
6492 return TASKS_DIR / f"{task_id}.json"
6593
6694
6795def create_task(subject: str, description: str = "",
6896 blockedBy: list[str] | None = None) -> Task:
6997 task = Task(
7098 id=f"task_{int(time.time())}_{random.randint(0, 9999):04d}",
7199 subject=subject, description=description,
72100 status="pending", owner=None,
73101 blockedBy=blockedBy or [],
74102 )
75103 save_task(task)
76104 return task
77105
78106
79107def save_task(task: Task):
80108 _task_path(task.id).write_text(json.dumps(asdict(task), indent=2))
81109
82110
83111def load_task(task_id: str) -> Task:
84112 return Task(**json.loads(_task_path(task_id).read_text()))
85113
86114
87115def list_tasks() -> list[Task]:
88116 return [Task(**json.loads(p.read_text()))
89117 for p in sorted(TASKS_DIR.glob("task_*.json"))]
90118
91119
92120def get_task_json(task_id: str) -> str:
93121 return json.dumps(asdict(load_task(task_id)), indent=2)
94122
95123
96124def can_start(task_id: str) -> bool:
125+ # Dependencies are intentionally simple: every blocker must exist and be
126+ # completed before the task can be claimed.
97127 task = load_task(task_id)
98128 for dep_id in task.blockedBy:
99129 if not _task_path(dep_id).exists():
100130 return False
101131 if load_task(dep_id).status != "completed":
102132 return False
103133 return True
104134
105135
106136def claim_task(task_id: str, owner: str = "agent") -> str:
107137 task = load_task(task_id)
108138 if task.status != "pending":
109139 return f"Task {task_id} is {task.status}, cannot claim"
110140 if task.owner:
111141 return f"Task {task_id} already owned by {task.owner}"
112142 if not can_start(task_id):
113143 deps = [d for d in task.blockedBy
114144 if _task_path(d).exists() and load_task(d).status != "completed"]
115145 missing = [d for d in task.blockedBy if not _task_path(d).exists()]
116146 parts = []
117147 if deps: parts.append(f"blocked by: {deps}")
118148 if missing: parts.append(f"missing deps: {missing}")
119149 return "Cannot start — " + ", ".join(parts)
120150 task.owner = owner
121151 task.status = "in_progress"
122152 save_task(task)
123153 print(f" \033[36m[claim] {task.subject} → in_progress\033[0m")
124154 return f"Claimed {task.id} ({task.subject})"
125155
126156
127157def complete_task(task_id: str) -> str:
128158 task = load_task(task_id)
129159 if task.status != "in_progress":
130160 return f"Task {task_id} is {task.status}, cannot complete"
131161 task.status = "completed"
132162 save_task(task)
133163 unblocked = [t.subject for t in list_tasks()
134164 if t.status == "pending" and t.blockedBy and can_start(t.id)]
135165 print(f" \033[32m[complete] {task.subject} ✓\033[0m")
136166 msg = f"Completed {task.id} ({task.subject})"
137167 if unblocked:
138168 msg += f"\nUnblocked: {', '.join(unblocked)}"
139169 return msg

141171
142172# ── Worktree System ──
143173
174+# Worktree names become filesystem paths, so the teaching version keeps the
175+# validation rules strict and reuses them for create/remove/keep.
144176WORKTREES_DIR = WORKDIR / ".worktrees"
145177WORKTREES_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
146178
147179VALID_WT_NAME = re.compile(r'^[A-Za-z0-9._-]{1,64}$')
148180
149181
150182def validate_worktree_name(name: str) -> str | None:
151183 if not name:
152184 return "Worktree name cannot be empty"
153185 if name in (".", ".."):
154186 return f"'{name}' is not a valid worktree name"
155187 if not VALID_WT_NAME.match(name):
156188 return (f"Invalid worktree name '{name}': "
157189 "only letters, digits, dots, underscores, dashes (1-64 chars)")
158190 return None
159191
160192
161193def run_git(args: list[str]) -> tuple[bool, str]:
162194 try:
163195 r = subprocess.run(["git"] + args, cwd=WORKDIR,
164196 capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=30)
165197 out = (r.stdout + r.stderr).strip()
166198 return r.returncode == 0, out[:5000] if out else "(no output)"
167199 except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
168200 return False, "Error: git timeout"
169201
170202
171203def log_event(event_type: str, worktree_name: str, task_id: str = ""):
172204 event = {"type": event_type, "worktree": worktree_name,
173205 "task_id": task_id, "ts": time.time()}
174206 events_file = WORKTREES_DIR / "events.jsonl"
175207 with open(events_file, "a") as f:
176208 f.write(json.dumps(event) + "\n")
177209
178210
179211def create_worktree(name: str, task_id: str = "") -> str:
212+ # Tool-layer validation is part of the safety boundary; do it before git
213+ # sees the name, not only after git happens to reject something.
180214 err = validate_worktree_name(name)
181215 if err:
182216 return f"Error: {err}"
217+ if task_id:
218+ try:
219+ load_task(task_id)
220+ except FileNotFoundError:
221+ return f"Error: task {task_id} not found"
183222 path = WORKTREES_DIR / name
184223 if path.exists():
185224 return f"Worktree '{name}' already exists at {path}"
186225 ok, result = run_git(["worktree", "add", str(path), "-b", f"wt/{name}", "HEAD"])
187226 if not ok:
188227 return f"Git error: {result}"
189228 if task_id:
190229 bind_task_to_worktree(task_id, name)
191230 log_event("create", name, task_id)
192231 print(f" \033[33m[worktree] created: {name} at {path}\033[0m")
193232 return f"Worktree '{name}' created at {path}"
194233
195234
196235def bind_task_to_worktree(task_id: str, worktree_name: str):
197236 task = load_task(task_id)
198237 task.worktree = worktree_name
199238 save_task(task)
200239
201240
202241def _count_worktree_changes(path: Path) -> tuple[int, int]:
203242 try:
204243 r1 = subprocess.run(["git", "status", "--porcelain"],
205244 cwd=path, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
206245 files = len([l for l in r1.stdout.strip().splitlines() if l.strip()])
207246 r2 = subprocess.run(["git", "log", "@{push}..HEAD", "--oneline"],
208247 cwd=path, capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10)
209248 commits = len([l for l in r2.stdout.strip().splitlines() if l.strip()])
210249 return files, commits
211250 except Exception:
212251 return -1, -1
213252
214253
215254def remove_worktree(name: str, discard_changes: bool = False) -> str:
216255 err = validate_worktree_name(name)
217256 if err:
218257 return err
219258 path = WORKTREES_DIR / name
220259 if not path.exists():
221260 return f"Worktree '{name}' not found"
222261 if not discard_changes:
223262 files, commits = _count_worktree_changes(path)
224263 if files < 0:
225264 return "Cannot verify status. Use discard_changes=true to force."
226265 if files > 0 or commits > 0:
227266 return (f"Worktree '{name}' has {files} file(s), {commits} commit(s). "
228267 "Use discard_changes=true or keep_worktree.")
229268 ok1, _ = run_git(["worktree", "remove", str(path), "--force"])
230269 if not ok1:
231270 return f"Failed to remove worktree '{name}'"
232271 run_git(["branch", "-D", f"wt/{name}"])
233272 log_event("remove", name)
234273 print(f" \033[33m[worktree] removed: {name}\033[0m")
235274 return f"Worktree '{name}' removed"
236275
237276
238277def keep_worktree(name: str) -> str:
239278 err = validate_worktree_name(name)
240279 if err:
241280 return err
242281 log_event("keep", name)
243282 return f"Worktree '{name}' kept for review (branch: wt/{name})"
244283
245284
285+# ── Skill Loading ──
286+
287+SKILL_REGISTRY: dict[str, dict] = {}
288+
289+
290+def _parse_frontmatter(text: str) -> tuple[dict, str]:
291+ if not text.startswith("---"):
292+ return {}, text
293+ parts = text.split("---", 2)
294+ if len(parts) < 3:
295+ return {}, text
296+ try:
297+ meta = yaml.safe_load(parts[1]) or {}
298+ except yaml.YAMLError:
299+ meta = {}
300+ return meta, parts[2].strip()
301+
302+
303+def scan_skills():
304+ SKILL_REGISTRY.clear()
305+ if not SKILLS_DIR.exists():
306+ return
307+ for directory in sorted(SKILLS_DIR.iterdir()):
308+ if not directory.is_dir():
309+ continue
310+ manifest = directory / "SKILL.md"
311+ if not manifest.exists():
312+ continue
313+ raw = manifest.read_text()
314+ meta, _ = _parse_frontmatter(raw)
315+ name = meta.get("name", directory.name)
316+ desc = meta.get("description", raw.split("\n")[0].lstrip("#").strip())
317+ SKILL_REGISTRY[name] = {
318+ "name": name,
319+ "description": desc,
320+ "content": raw,
321+ }
322+
323+
324+scan_skills()
325+
326+
327+def list_skills() -> str:
328+ if not SKILL_REGISTRY:
329+ return "(no skills found)"
330+ return "\n".join(
331+ f"- {skill['name']}: {skill['description']}"
332+ for skill in SKILL_REGISTRY.values())
333+
334+
335+def load_skill(name: str) -> str:
336+ skill = SKILL_REGISTRY.get(name)
337+ if not skill:
338+ available = ", ".join(SKILL_REGISTRY.keys()) or "(none)"
339+ return f"Skill not found: {name}. Available: {available}"
340+ return skill["content"]
341+
342+
246343# ── Prompt Assembly ──
247344
248345PROMPT_SECTIONS = {
249346 "identity": "You are a coding agent. Act, don't explain.",
250- "tools": "Available tools: bash, read_file, write_file, "
347+ "tools": "Available tools: bash, read_file, write_file, edit_file, glob, "
348+ "todo_write, task, load_skill, compact, "
251349 "create_task, list_tasks, get_task, claim_task, complete_task, "
350+ "schedule_cron, list_crons, cancel_cron, "
252351 "spawn_teammate, send_message, check_inbox, "
253352 "request_shutdown, request_plan, review_plan, "
254353 "create_worktree, remove_worktree, keep_worktree, "
255354 "connect_mcp. MCP tools are prefixed mcp__{server}__{tool}.",
256355 "workspace": f"Working directory: {WORKDIR}",
257356 "memory": "Relevant memories are injected below when available.",
258357}
259358
260359
261360def assemble_system_prompt(context: dict) -> str:
361+ # The system prompt is rebuilt each turn from live context. This is where
362+ # memory, skill catalog, MCP state, and active teammates become visible.
262363 sections = [PROMPT_SECTIONS["identity"],
263364 PROMPT_SECTIONS["tools"],
264365 PROMPT_SECTIONS["workspace"]]
366+ sections.append(f"Current time: {datetime.now().isoformat(timespec='seconds')}")
367+ sections.append("Skills catalog:\n" + list_skills() +
368+ "\nUse load_skill(name) when a skill is relevant.")
265369 if context.get("memories"):
266370 sections.append(f"Relevant memories:\n{context['memories']}")
267371 mcp_names = list(mcp_clients.keys())
268372 if mcp_names:
269373 sections.append(f"Connected MCP servers: {', '.join(mcp_names)}")
270374 return "\n\n".join(sections)

272376
273377# ── Basic Tools ──
274378
275379def safe_path(p: str, cwd: Path = None) -> Path:
380+ # File tools stay inside the workspace or teammate worktree. Bash remains
381+ # powerful on purpose and is controlled by the permission hook instead.
276382 base = cwd or WORKDIR
277383 path = (base / p).resolve()
278384 if not path.is_relative_to(base):
279385 raise ValueError(f"Path escapes workspace: {p}")
280386 return path
281387
282388
283-def run_bash(command: str, cwd: Path = None) -> str:
389+def run_bash(command: str, cwd: Path = None,
390+ run_in_background: bool = False) -> str:
391+ # run_in_background is consumed by the dispatcher; direct execution ignores it.
284392 try:
285393 r = subprocess.run(command, shell=True, cwd=cwd or WORKDIR,
286394 capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=120)
287395 out = (r.stdout + r.stderr).strip()
288396 return out[:50000] if out else "(no output)"
289397 except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
290398 return "Error: Timeout (120s)"
291399
292400
293-def run_read(path: str, limit: int | None = None, cwd: Path = None) -> str:
401+def run_read(path: str, limit: int | None = None,
402+ offset: int = 0, cwd: Path = None) -> str:
294403 try:
295404 lines = safe_path(path, cwd).read_text().splitlines()
296- if limit and limit < len(lines):
405+ offset = max(int(offset or 0), 0)
406+ limit = int(limit) if limit is not None else None
407+ lines = lines[offset:]
408+ if limit is not None and limit < len(lines):
297409 lines = lines[:limit] + [f"... ({len(lines) - limit} more lines)"]
298410 return "\n".join(lines)
299411 except Exception as e:
300412 return f"Error: {e}"
301413
302414
303415def run_write(path: str, content: str, cwd: Path = None) -> str:
304416 try:
305417 fp = safe_path(path, cwd)
306418 fp.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
307419 fp.write_text(content)
308420 return f"Wrote {len(content)} bytes to {path}"
309421 except Exception as e:
310422 return f"Error: {e}"
311423
312424
425+def run_edit(path: str, old_text: str, new_text: str,
426+ cwd: Path = None) -> str:
427+ try:
428+ fp = safe_path(path, cwd)
429+ text = fp.read_text()
430+ if old_text not in text:
431+ return f"Error: text not found in {path}"
432+ fp.write_text(text.replace(old_text, new_text, 1))
433+ return f"Edited {path}"
434+ except Exception as e:
435+ return f"Error: {e}"
436+
437+
438+def run_glob(pattern: str, cwd: Path = None) -> str:
439+ import glob as g
440+ try:
441+ base = cwd or WORKDIR
442+ results = []
443+ for match in g.glob(pattern, root_dir=base):
444+ if (base / match).resolve().is_relative_to(base):
445+ results.append(match)
446+ return "\n".join(results) if results else "(no matches)"
447+ except Exception as e:
448+ return f"Error: {e}"
449+
450+
451+def call_tool_handler(handler, args: dict, name: str) -> str:
452+ if not handler:
453+ return f"Unknown: {name}"
454+ try:
455+ return handler(**(args or {}))
456+ except TypeError as e:
457+ return f"Error: {e}"
458+
459+
460+def _normalize_todos(todos):
461+ if isinstance(todos, str):
462+ try:
463+ todos = json.loads(todos)
464+ except json.JSONDecodeError:
465+ try:
466+ todos = ast.literal_eval(todos)
467+ except (SyntaxError, ValueError):
468+ return None, "Error: todos must be a list or JSON array string"
469+ if not isinstance(todos, list):
470+ return None, "Error: todos must be a list"
471+ for i, todo in enumerate(todos):
472+ if not isinstance(todo, dict):
473+ return None, f"Error: todos[{i}] must be an object"
474+ if "content" not in todo or "status" not in todo:
475+ return None, f"Error: todos[{i}] missing 'content' or 'status'"
476+ if todo["status"] not in ("pending", "in_progress", "completed"):
477+ return None, f"Error: todos[{i}] has invalid status '{todo['status']}'"
478+ return todos, None
479+
480+def run_todo_write(todos: list) -> str:
481+ global CURRENT_TODOS
482+ todos, error = _normalize_todos(todos)
483+ if error:
484+ return error
485+ CURRENT_TODOS = todos
486+ print(f" \033[33m[todo] updated {len(CURRENT_TODOS)} item(s)\033[0m")
487+ return f"Updated {len(CURRENT_TODOS)} todos"
488+
489+
313490# ── MessageBus ──
314491
492+# Team communication is append-only JSONL mailboxes. This keeps the protocol
493+# inspectable on disk and lets background teammates send messages.
315494MAILBOX_DIR = WORKDIR / ".mailboxes"
316495MAILBOX_DIR.mkdir(exist_ok=True)
317496
318497
319498class MessageBus:
320499 def send(self, from_agent: str, to_agent: str, content: str,
321500 msg_type: str = "message", metadata: dict = None):
322501 msg = {"from": from_agent, "to": to_agent,
323502 "content": content, "type": msg_type,
324503 "ts": time.time(), "metadata": metadata or {}}
325504 inbox = MAILBOX_DIR / f"{to_agent}.jsonl"
326505 with open(inbox, "a") as f:
327506 f.write(json.dumps(msg) + "\n")
328- print(f" \033[33m[bus] {from_agent} → {to_agent}: "
329- f"({msg_type}) {content[:50]}\033[0m")
507+ terminal_print(f" \033[33m[bus] {from_agent} → {to_agent}: "
508+ f"({msg_type}) {content[:50]}\033[0m")
330509
331510 def read_inbox(self, agent: str) -> list[dict]:
332511 inbox = MAILBOX_DIR / f"{agent}.jsonl"
333512 if not inbox.exists():
334513 return []
335514 msgs = [json.loads(line) for line in inbox.read_text().splitlines()
336515 if line.strip()]
337516 inbox.unlink()
338517 return msgs
339518
340519
341520BUS = MessageBus()
342521active_teammates: dict[str, bool] = {}
343522
344523# ── Protocol State ──
345524
346525@dataclass
347526class ProtocolState:
348527 request_id: str
349528 type: str
350529 sender: str
351530 target: str
352531 status: str
353532 payload: str
354533 created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
355534
356535
357536pending_requests: dict[str, ProtocolState] = {}
358537
359538
360539def new_request_id() -> str:
361540 return f"req_{random.randint(0, 999999):06d}"
362541
363542
364543def match_response(response_type: str, request_id: str, approve: bool):
544+ # Responses are matched by request_id so one protocol reply cannot approve
545+ # a different pending request.
365546 state = pending_requests.get(request_id)
366547 if not state:
367548 return
368549 if state.type == "shutdown" and response_type != "shutdown_response":
369550 return
370551 if state.type == "plan_approval" and response_type != "plan_approval_response":
371552 return
372553 state.status = "approved" if approve else "rejected"
373554
374555
375556def consume_lead_inbox(route_protocol=True) -> list[dict]:
376557 msgs = BUS.read_inbox("lead")
377558 if route_protocol:
378559 for msg in msgs:
379560 meta = msg.get("metadata", {})
380561 req_id = meta.get("request_id", "")
381562 msg_type = msg.get("type", "")
382563 if req_id and msg_type.endswith("_response"):
383564 match_response(msg_type, req_id, meta.get("approve", False))
384565 return msgs
385566
386567
387568# ── Autonomous Agent ──
388569
389570IDLE_POLL_INTERVAL = 5
390571IDLE_TIMEOUT = 60
391572
392573
393574def scan_unclaimed_tasks() -> list[dict]:
394575 unclaimed = []
395576 for f in sorted(TASKS_DIR.glob("task_*.json")):
396577 task = json.loads(f.read_text())
397578 if (task.get("status") == "pending"
398579 and not task.get("owner")
399580 and can_start(task["id"])):
400581 unclaimed.append(task)
401582 return unclaimed
402583
403584
404585def idle_poll(agent_name: str, messages: list,
405- name: str, role: str) -> str:
586+ name: str, role: str,
587+ worktree_context: dict | None = None) -> str:
588+ # Autonomous teammates wake up for inbox messages first, then look for
589+ # unclaimed tasks. This keeps direct protocol messages higher priority.
406590 for _ in range(IDLE_TIMEOUT // IDLE_POLL_INTERVAL):
407591 time.sleep(IDLE_POLL_INTERVAL)
408592 inbox = BUS.read_inbox(agent_name)
409593 if inbox:
410594 for msg in inbox:
411595 if msg.get("type") == "shutdown_request":
412596 req_id = msg.get("metadata", {}).get("request_id", "")
413597 BUS.send(name, "lead", "Shutting down.",
414598 "shutdown_response",
415599 {"request_id": req_id, "approve": True})
416600 return "shutdown"
417601 messages.append({"role": "user",
418602 "content": "<inbox>" + json.dumps(inbox) + "</inbox>"})
419603 return "work"
420604 unclaimed = scan_unclaimed_tasks()
421605 if unclaimed:
422606 task_data = unclaimed[0]
423607 result = claim_task(task_data["id"], agent_name)
424608 if "Claimed" in result:
425609 wt_info = ""
426610 if task_data.get("worktree"):
427- wt_info = f"\nWork directory: {WORKTREES_DIR / task_data['worktree']}"
611+ wt_path = WORKTREES_DIR / task_data["worktree"]
612+ wt_info = f"\nWork directory: {wt_path}"
613+ if worktree_context is not None:
614+ worktree_context["path"] = str(wt_path)
428615 messages.append({"role": "user",
429616 "content": f"<auto-claimed>Task {task_data['id']}: "
430617 f"{task_data['subject']}{wt_info}</auto-claimed>"})
431618 return "work"
432619 return "timeout"
433620
434621
435622# ── Teammate Thread ──
436623
437624def spawn_teammate_thread(name: str, role: str, prompt: str) -> str:
438625 if name in active_teammates:
439626 return f"Teammate '{name}' already exists"
440627
628+ # Plan approval is a real gate: after submit_plan, the teammate stops
629+ # taking model/tool steps until lead sends plan_approval_response.
630+ protocol_ctx = {"waiting_plan": None}
441631 system = (f"You are '{name}', a {role}. "
442632 f"Use tools to complete tasks. "
443633 f"If a task has a worktree, work in that directory.")
444634
445635 def handle_inbox_message(name: str, msg: dict, messages: list):
446636 msg_type = msg.get("type", "message")
447637 meta = msg.get("metadata", {})
448638 req_id = meta.get("request_id", "")
449639 if msg_type == "shutdown_request":
450640 BUS.send(name, "lead", "Shutting down.",
451641 "shutdown_response",
452642 {"request_id": req_id, "approve": True})
453643 return True
454644 if msg_type == "plan_approval_response":
455645 approve = meta.get("approve", False)
646+ if req_id == protocol_ctx["waiting_plan"]:
647+ protocol_ctx["waiting_plan"] = None
456648 messages.append({"role": "user",
457649 "content": "[Plan approved]" if approve
458650 else f"[Plan rejected] {msg['content']}"})
459651 return False
460652
461653 def run():
462654 wt_ctx = {"path": None}
463655
464656 def _wt_cwd():
657+ # Once a task with a worktree is claimed, all teammate file tools
658+ # transparently run inside that isolated directory.
465659 p = wt_ctx["path"]
466660 return Path(p) if p else None
467661
468662 def _run_bash(command: str) -> str:
469663 return run_bash(command, cwd=_wt_cwd())
470664
471665 def _run_read(path: str) -> str:
472666 return run_read(path, cwd=_wt_cwd())
473667
474668 def _run_write(path: str, content: str) -> str:
475669 return run_write(path, content, cwd=_wt_cwd())
476670
477671 def _run_list_tasks():
478672 tasks = list_tasks()
479673 if not tasks:
480674 return "No tasks."
481675 return "\n".join(
482676 f" {t.id}: {t.subject} [{t.status}]"
483677 + (f" (wt:{t.worktree})" if t.worktree else "")
484678 for t in tasks)
485679
486680 def _run_claim_task(task_id: str):
487681 result = claim_task(task_id, owner=name)
488682 if "Claimed" in result:
489683 task = load_task(task_id)
490684 wt_ctx["path"] = (str(WORKTREES_DIR / task.worktree)
491685 if task.worktree else None)
492686 return result
493687
494688 def _run_complete_task(task_id: str):
495689 result = complete_task(task_id)
496690 wt_ctx["path"] = None
497691 return result
498692
499693 messages = [{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
500694 sub_tools = [
501695 {"name": "bash", "description": "Run a shell command.",
502696 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
503697 "properties": {"command": {"type": "string"}},
504698 "required": ["command"]}},
505699 {"name": "read_file", "description": "Read file.",
506700 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
507- "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"}},
701+ "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"},
702+ "limit": {"type": "integer"},
703+ "offset": {"type": "integer"}},
508704 "required": ["path"]}},
509705 {"name": "write_file", "description": "Write file.",
510706 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
511707 "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"},
512708 "content": {"type": "string"}},
513709 "required": ["path", "content"]}},
514710 {"name": "send_message",
515711 "description": "Send message to another agent.",
516712 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
517713 "properties": {"to": {"type": "string"},
518714 "content": {"type": "string"}},
519715 "required": ["to", "content"]}},
520716 {"name": "submit_plan",
521717 "description": "Submit a plan for Lead approval.",
522718 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
523719 "properties": {"plan": {"type": "string"}},
524720 "required": ["plan"]}},
525721 {"name": "list_tasks",
526722 "description": "List all tasks.",
527723 "input_schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {},
528724 "required": []}},
529725 {"name": "claim_task",
530726 "description": "Claim a pending task.",
531727 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
532728 "properties": {"task_id": {"type": "string"}},
533729 "required": ["task_id"]}},
534730 {"name": "complete_task",
535731 "description": "Mark an in-progress task as completed.",
536732 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
537733 "properties": {"task_id": {"type": "string"}},
538734 "required": ["task_id"]}},
539735 ]
540736
541737 sub_handlers = {
542738 "bash": _run_bash, "read_file": _run_read,
543739 "write_file": _run_write,
544740 "send_message": lambda to, content: (BUS.send(name, to, content),
545741 "Sent")[1],
546- "submit_plan": lambda plan: _teammate_submit_plan(name, plan),
547742 "list_tasks": _run_list_tasks,
548743 "claim_task": _run_claim_task,
549744 "complete_task": _run_complete_task,
550745 }
551746
552747 while True:
553748 if len(messages) <= 3:
554749 messages.insert(0, {"role": "user",
555750 "content": f"<identity>You are '{name}', role: {role}. "
556751 f"Continue your work.</identity>"})
557752 should_shutdown = False
558753 for _ in range(10):
559754 inbox = BUS.read_inbox(name)
560755 for msg in inbox:
561756 stopped = handle_inbox_message(name, msg, messages)
562757 if stopped:
563758 should_shutdown = True
564759 break
565760 if should_shutdown:
566761 break
762+ if protocol_ctx["waiting_plan"]:
763+ # Poll only for protocol replies while the approval gate is
764+ # closed; do not let the model continue with the task.
765+ time.sleep(IDLE_POLL_INTERVAL)
766+ continue
567767 if inbox and not should_shutdown:
568768 non_protocol = [m for m in inbox
569769 if m.get("type") == "message"]
570770 if non_protocol:
571771 messages.append({"role": "user",
572772 "content": "<inbox>" + json.dumps(non_protocol) + "</inbox>"})
573773 try:
574774 response = client.messages.create(
575775 model=MODEL, system=system, messages=messages[-20:],
576776 tools=sub_tools, max_tokens=8000)
577777 except Exception:
578778 break
579779 messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response.content})
580- if response.stop_reason != "tool_use":
780+ if not has_tool_use(response.content):
581781 break
582782 results = []
583783 for block in response.content:
584784 if block.type == "tool_use":
585- handler = sub_handlers.get(block.name)
586- output = handler(**block.input) if handler else "Unknown"
785+ if block.name == "submit_plan":
786+ output = _teammate_submit_plan(
787+ name, block.input.get("plan", ""))
788+ match = re.search(r"\((req_\d+)\)", output)
789+ protocol_ctx["waiting_plan"] = (
790+ match.group(1) if match else output)
791+ else:
792+ handler = sub_handlers.get(block.name)
793+ output = call_tool_handler(handler, block.input,
794+ block.name)
587795 results.append({"type": "tool_result",
588796 "tool_use_id": block.id,
589797 "content": str(output)})
798+ if protocol_ctx["waiting_plan"]:
799+ # Ignore later tool_use blocks from the same model
800+ # response; they belong after approval, not before.
801+ break
590802 messages.append({"role": "user", "content": results})
803+ if protocol_ctx["waiting_plan"]:
804+ break
591805 if should_shutdown:
592806 break
593- idle_result = idle_poll(name, messages, name, role)
807+ if protocol_ctx["waiting_plan"]:
808+ continue
809+ idle_result = idle_poll(name, messages, name, role, wt_ctx)
594810 if idle_result in ("shutdown", "timeout"):
595811 break
596812
597813 summary = "Done."
598814 for msg in reversed(messages):
599815 if msg["role"] == "assistant" and isinstance(msg["content"], list):
600816 for b in msg["content"]:
601817 if getattr(b, "type", None) == "text":
602818 summary = b.text
603819 break
604820 else:
605821 continue
606822 break
607823 BUS.send(name, "lead", summary, "result")
608824 active_teammates.pop(name, None)
609825
610826 active_teammates[name] = True
611827 threading.Thread(target=run, daemon=True).start()
612828 return f"Teammate '{name}' spawned as {role}"
613829
614830
615831def _teammate_submit_plan(from_name: str, plan: str) -> str:
616832 req_id = new_request_id()
617833 pending_requests[req_id] = ProtocolState(
618834 request_id=req_id, type="plan_approval",
619835 sender=from_name, target="lead",
620836 status="pending", payload=plan)
621837 BUS.send(from_name, "lead", plan,
622838 "plan_approval_request",
623839 {"request_id": req_id})
624840 return f"Plan submitted ({req_id})"
625841
626842
627843# ── Lead Protocol Tools ──
628844
629845def run_request_shutdown(teammate: str) -> str:
630846 req_id = new_request_id()
631847 pending_requests[req_id] = ProtocolState(
632848 request_id=req_id, type="shutdown",
633849 sender="lead", target=teammate,
634850 status="pending", payload="")
635851 BUS.send("lead", teammate, "Shut down.", "shutdown_request",
636852 {"request_id": req_id})
637853 return f"Shutdown request sent to {teammate}"
638854
639855
640856def run_request_plan(teammate: str, task: str) -> str:
641857 BUS.send("lead", teammate, f"Submit plan for: {task}", "message")
642858 return f"Asked {teammate} to submit a plan"
643859
644860
645861def run_review_plan(request_id: str, approve: bool,
646862 feedback: str = "") -> str:
647863 state = pending_requests.get(request_id)
648864 if not state:
649865 return f"Request {request_id} not found"
650866 state.status = "approved" if approve else "rejected"
651867 BUS.send("lead", state.sender,
652868 feedback or ("Approved" if approve else "Rejected"),
653869 "plan_approval_response",
654870 {"request_id": request_id, "approve": approve})
655871 return f"Plan {'approved' if approve else 'rejected'}"
656872
657873
658-# ── MCP System (s19 new) ──
874+# ── Hooks + Permission Pipeline ──
659875
876+# Hooks are intentionally outside tool handlers. The loop can add permission,
877+# logging, and stop behavior without changing each individual tool.
878+HOOKS = {"UserPromptSubmit": [], "PreToolUse": [],
879+ "PostToolUse": [], "Stop": []}
880+
881+
882+def register_hook(event: str, callback):
883+ HOOKS[event].append(callback)
884+
885+
886+def trigger_hooks(event: str, *args):
887+ for callback in HOOKS[event]:
888+ result = callback(*args)
889+ if result is not None:
890+ return result
891+ return None
892+
893+
894+DENY_LIST = ["rm -rf /", "sudo", "shutdown", "reboot", "mkfs", "dd if="]
895+DESTRUCTIVE = ["rm ", "> /etc/", "chmod 777"]
896+
897+
898+def permission_hook(block):
899+ # The permission layer sees the raw tool_use before dispatch. It can deny,
900+ # ask the user, or allow execution to continue.
901+ if block.name == "bash":
902+ command = block.input.get("command", "")
903+ for pattern in DENY_LIST:
904+ if pattern in command:
905+ return f"Permission denied: '{pattern}' is on the deny list"
906+ if any(token in command for token in DESTRUCTIVE):
907+ print(f"\n\033[33m[permission] destructive command\033[0m")
908+ print(f" {command}")
909+ choice = input(" Allow? [y/N] ").strip().lower()
910+ if choice not in ("y", "yes"):
911+ return "Permission denied by user"
912+ if block.name in ("write_file", "edit_file"):
913+ path = block.input.get("path", "")
914+ try:
915+ safe_path(path)
916+ except Exception:
917+ return f"Permission denied: path escapes workspace: {path}"
918+ if block.name.startswith("mcp__") and "deploy" in block.name:
919+ print(f"\n\033[33m[permission] MCP destructive-looking tool: {block.name}\033[0m")
920+ choice = input(" Allow? [y/N] ").strip().lower()
921+ if choice not in ("y", "yes"):
922+ return "Permission denied by user"
923+ return None
924+
925+
926+def log_hook(block):
927+ print(f"\033[90m[HOOK] {block.name}\033[0m")
928+ return None
929+
930+
931+def large_output_hook(block, output):
932+ if len(str(output)) > 100000:
933+ print(f"\033[33m[HOOK] large output from {block.name}: "
934+ f"{len(str(output))} chars\033[0m")
935+ return None
936+
937+
938+def user_prompt_hook(query: str):
939+ print(f"\033[90m[HOOK] UserPromptSubmit: {WORKDIR}\033[0m")
940+ return None
941+
942+
943+def stop_hook(messages: list):
944+ tool_count = 0
945+ for msg in messages:
946+ content = msg.get("content")
947+ if isinstance(content, list):
948+ tool_count += sum(1 for item in content
949+ if isinstance(item, dict)
950+ and item.get("type") == "tool_result")
951+ print(f"\033[90m[HOOK] Stop: {tool_count} tool result(s)\033[0m")
952+ return None
953+
954+
955+register_hook("UserPromptSubmit", user_prompt_hook)
956+register_hook("PreToolUse", permission_hook)
957+register_hook("PreToolUse", log_hook)
958+register_hook("PostToolUse", large_output_hook)
959+register_hook("Stop", stop_hook)
960+
961+
962+# ── Subagent Tool ──
963+
964+SUB_SYSTEM = (
965+ f"You are a coding subagent at {WORKDIR}. "
966+ "Complete the task, then return a concise final summary. "
967+ "Do not spawn more agents."
968+)
969+
970+
971+SUB_TOOLS = [
972+ {"name": "bash", "description": "Run a shell command.",
973+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
974+ "properties": {"command": {"type": "string"}},
975+ "required": ["command"]}},
976+ {"name": "read_file", "description": "Read file contents.",
977+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
978+ "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"},
979+ "limit": {"type": "integer"},
980+ "offset": {"type": "integer"}},
981+ "required": ["path"]}},
982+ {"name": "write_file", "description": "Write content to a file.",
983+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
984+ "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"},
985+ "content": {"type": "string"}},
986+ "required": ["path", "content"]}},
987+ {"name": "edit_file", "description": "Replace exact text in a file once.",
988+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
989+ "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"},
990+ "old_text": {"type": "string"},
991+ "new_text": {"type": "string"}},
992+ "required": ["path", "old_text", "new_text"]}},
993+ {"name": "glob", "description": "Find files matching a glob pattern.",
994+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
995+ "properties": {"pattern": {"type": "string"}},
996+ "required": ["pattern"]}},
997+]
998+
999+
1000+SUB_HANDLERS = {
1001+ "bash": run_bash, "read_file": run_read,
1002+ "write_file": run_write, "edit_file": run_edit,
1003+ "glob": run_glob,
1004+}
1005+
1006+
1007+def extract_text(content) -> str:
1008+ if not isinstance(content, list):
1009+ return str(content)
1010+ return "\n".join(
1011+ getattr(block, "text", "")
1012+ for block in content
1013+ if getattr(block, "type", None) == "text").strip()
1014+
1015+
1016+def has_tool_use(content) -> bool:
1017+ # Do not rely on stop_reason alone; the concrete tool_use block is the
1018+ # continuation signal used by the loop.
1019+ return any(getattr(block, "type", None) == "tool_use"
1020+ for block in content)
1021+
1022+
1023+def spawn_subagent(description: str) -> str:
1024+ messages = [{"role": "user", "content": description}]
1025+ for _ in range(30):
1026+ response = client.messages.create(
1027+ model=MODEL, system=SUB_SYSTEM, messages=messages,
1028+ tools=SUB_TOOLS, max_tokens=8000)
1029+ messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response.content})
1030+ if not has_tool_use(response.content):
1031+ break
1032+ results = []
1033+ for block in response.content:
1034+ if block.type != "tool_use":
1035+ continue
1036+ blocked = trigger_hooks("PreToolUse", block)
1037+ if blocked:
1038+ output = str(blocked)
1039+ else:
1040+ handler = SUB_HANDLERS.get(block.name)
1041+ output = call_tool_handler(handler, block.input, block.name)
1042+ trigger_hooks("PostToolUse", block, output)
1043+ results.append({"type": "tool_result",
1044+ "tool_use_id": block.id,
1045+ "content": str(output)})
1046+ messages.append({"role": "user", "content": results})
1047+ for msg in reversed(messages):
1048+ if msg["role"] == "assistant":
1049+ text = extract_text(msg["content"])
1050+ if text:
1051+ return text
1052+ return "Subagent finished without a text summary."
1053+
1054+
1055+# ── Context Compaction ──
1056+
1057+# Compaction is layered: first shrink oversized tool results, then trim old
1058+# message ranges, and only call the model for a summary when the context is
1059+# still too large or the model explicitly asks for compact.
1060+def estimate_size(messages: list) -> int:
1061+ return len(json.dumps(messages, default=str))
1062+
1063+def block_type(block):
1064+ return block.get("type") if isinstance(block, dict) else getattr(block, "type", None)
1065+
1066+
1067+def message_has_tool_use(message: dict) -> bool:
1068+ if message.get("role") != "assistant":
1069+ return False
1070+ content = message.get("content")
1071+ if not isinstance(content, list):
1072+ return False
1073+ return any(block_type(block) == "tool_use" for block in content)
1074+
1075+
1076+def is_tool_result_message(message: dict) -> bool:
1077+ if message.get("role") != "user":
1078+ return False
1079+ content = message.get("content")
1080+ if not isinstance(content, list):
1081+ return False
1082+ return any(isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_result"
1083+ for block in content)
1084+
1085+
1086+def collect_tool_results(messages: list):
1087+ found = []
1088+ for mi, msg in enumerate(messages):
1089+ content = msg.get("content")
1090+ if msg.get("role") != "user" or not isinstance(content, list):
1091+ continue
1092+ for bi, block in enumerate(content):
1093+ if isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "tool_result":
1094+ found.append((mi, bi, block))
1095+ return found
1096+
1097+
1098+def persist_large_output(tool_use_id: str, output: str) -> str:
1099+ if len(output) <= PERSIST_THRESHOLD:
1100+ return output
1101+ TOOL_RESULTS_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1102+ path = TOOL_RESULTS_DIR / f"{tool_use_id}.txt"
1103+ if not path.exists():
1104+ path.write_text(output)
1105+ return (f"<persisted-output>\nFull output: {path}\n"
1106+ f"Preview:\n{output[:2000]}\n</persisted-output>")
1107+
1108+
1109+def tool_result_budget(messages: list, max_bytes: int = 200_000) -> list:
1110+ if not messages:
1111+ return messages
1112+ last = messages[-1]
1113+ content = last.get("content")
1114+ if last.get("role") != "user" or not isinstance(content, list):
1115+ return messages
1116+ blocks = [(i, b) for i, b in enumerate(content)
1117+ if isinstance(b, dict) and b.get("type") == "tool_result"]
1118+ total = sum(len(str(b.get("content", ""))) for _, b in blocks)
1119+ if total <= max_bytes:
1120+ return messages
1121+ for _, block in sorted(blocks,
1122+ key=lambda pair: len(str(pair[1].get("content", ""))),
1123+ reverse=True):
1124+ if total <= max_bytes:
1125+ break
1126+ text = str(block.get("content", ""))
1127+ block["content"] = persist_large_output(
1128+ block.get("tool_use_id", "unknown"), text)
1129+ total = sum(len(str(b.get("content", ""))) for _, b in blocks)
1130+ return messages
1131+
1132+
1133+def snip_compact(messages: list, max_messages: int = 50) -> list:
1134+ if len(messages) <= max_messages:
1135+ return messages
1136+ head_end, tail_start = 3, len(messages) - (max_messages - 3)
1137+ if head_end > 0 and message_has_tool_use(messages[head_end - 1]):
1138+ while head_end < len(messages) and is_tool_result_message(messages[head_end]):
1139+ head_end += 1
1140+ if (tail_start > 0 and tail_start < len(messages)
1141+ and is_tool_result_message(messages[tail_start])
1142+ and message_has_tool_use(messages[tail_start - 1])):
1143+ tail_start -= 1
1144+ if head_end >= tail_start:
1145+ return messages
1146+ snipped = tail_start - head_end
1147+ return (messages[:head_end]
1148+ + [{"role": "user", "content": f"[snipped {snipped} messages]"}]
1149+ + messages[tail_start:])
1150+
1151+
1152+def micro_compact(messages: list) -> list:
1153+ tool_results = collect_tool_results(messages)
1154+ if len(tool_results) <= KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_RESULTS:
1155+ return messages
1156+ for _, _, block in tool_results[:-KEEP_RECENT_TOOL_RESULTS]:
1157+ if len(str(block.get("content", ""))) > 120:
1158+ block["content"] = "[Earlier tool result compacted. Re-run if needed.]"
1159+ return messages
1160+
1161+
1162+def write_transcript(messages: list) -> Path:
1163+ TRANSCRIPT_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
1164+ path = TRANSCRIPT_DIR / f"transcript_{int(time.time())}.jsonl"
1165+ with path.open("w") as f:
1166+ for msg in messages:
1167+ f.write(json.dumps(msg, default=str) + "\n")
1168+ return path
1169+
1170+
1171+def summarize_history(messages: list) -> str:
1172+ conversation = json.dumps(messages, default=str)[:80000]
1173+ prompt = ("Summarize this coding-agent conversation so work can continue. "
1174+ "Preserve current goal, key findings, changed files, remaining work, "
1175+ "and user constraints.\n\n" + conversation)
1176+ response = client.messages.create(
1177+ model=MODEL,
1178+ messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}],
1179+ max_tokens=2000)
1180+ return extract_text(response.content) or "(empty summary)"
1181+
1182+
1183+def compact_history(messages: list) -> list:
1184+ transcript = write_transcript(messages)
1185+ print(f" \033[36m[compact] transcript saved: {transcript}\033[0m")
1186+ summary = summarize_history(messages)
1187+ return [{"role": "user", "content": f"[Compacted]\n\n{summary}"}]
1188+
1189+
1190+def reactive_compact(messages: list) -> list:
1191+ transcript = write_transcript(messages)
1192+ print(f" \033[31m[reactive compact] transcript saved: {transcript}\033[0m")
1193+ tail_start = max(0, len(messages) - 5)
1194+ if (tail_start > 0 and tail_start < len(messages)
1195+ and is_tool_result_message(messages[tail_start])
1196+ and message_has_tool_use(messages[tail_start - 1])):
1197+ tail_start -= 1
1198+ try:
1199+ summary = summarize_history(messages[:tail_start])
1200+ except Exception:
1201+ summary = "Earlier conversation was trimmed after a prompt-too-long error."
1202+ return [{"role": "user", "content": f"[Reactive compact]\n\n{summary}"},
1203+ *messages[tail_start:]]
1204+
1205+
1206+# ── Error Recovery ──
1207+
1208+class RecoveryState:
1209+ def __init__(self):
1210+ self.has_escalated = False
1211+ self.recovery_count = 0
1212+ self.consecutive_529 = 0
1213+ self.has_attempted_reactive_compact = False
1214+ self.current_model = PRIMARY_MODEL
1215+
1216+
1217+def retry_delay(attempt: int) -> float:
1218+ base = min(BASE_DELAY_MS * (2 ** attempt), 32000) / 1000
1219+ return base + random.uniform(0, base * 0.25)
1220+
1221+
1222+def with_retry(fn, state: RecoveryState):
1223+ for attempt in range(MAX_RETRIES):
1224+ try:
1225+ result = fn()
1226+ state.consecutive_529 = 0
1227+ return result
1228+ except Exception as e:
1229+ name = type(e).__name__.lower()
1230+ msg = str(e).lower()
1231+ if "ratelimit" in name or "429" in msg:
1232+ delay = retry_delay(attempt)
1233+ print(f" \033[33m[429] retry {attempt + 1}/{MAX_RETRIES} "
1234+ f"after {delay:.1f}s\033[0m")
1235+ time.sleep(delay)
1236+ continue
1237+ if "overloaded" in name or "529" in msg or "overloaded" in msg:
1238+ state.consecutive_529 += 1
1239+ if state.consecutive_529 >= MAX_CONSECUTIVE_529 and FALLBACK_MODEL:
1240+ state.current_model = FALLBACK_MODEL
1241+ state.consecutive_529 = 0
1242+ print(f" \033[31m[529] switching to {FALLBACK_MODEL}\033[0m")
1243+ delay = retry_delay(attempt)
1244+ print(f" \033[33m[529] retry {attempt + 1}/{MAX_RETRIES} "
1245+ f"after {delay:.1f}s\033[0m")
1246+ time.sleep(delay)
1247+ continue
1248+ raise
1249+ raise RuntimeError(f"Max retries ({MAX_RETRIES}) exceeded")
1250+
1251+
1252+def is_prompt_too_long_error(e: Exception) -> bool:
1253+ msg = str(e).lower()
1254+ return (("prompt" in msg and "long" in msg)
1255+ or "context_length_exceeded" in msg
1256+ or "max_context_window" in msg)
1257+
1258+
1259+# ── Background Tasks ──
1260+
1261+# Slow tools return a placeholder tool_result immediately. Their real output is
1262+# later injected as a task_notification, so the main loop can keep moving.
1263+_bg_counter = 0
1264+background_tasks: dict[str, dict] = {}
1265+background_results: dict[str, str] = {}
1266+background_lock = threading.Lock()
1267+
1268+
1269+def is_slow_operation(tool_name: str, tool_input: dict) -> bool:
1270+ if tool_name != "bash":
1271+ return False
1272+ command = tool_input.get("command", "").lower()
1273+ slow_keywords = ["install", "build", "test", "deploy", "compile",
1274+ "docker build", "pip install", "npm install",
1275+ "cargo build", "pytest", "make"]
1276+ return any(keyword in command for keyword in slow_keywords)
1277+
1278+
1279+def should_run_background(tool_name: str, tool_input: dict) -> bool:
1280+ if tool_name != "bash":
1281+ return False
1282+ return bool(tool_input.get("run_in_background")) or is_slow_operation(tool_name, tool_input)
1283+
1284+
1285+def start_background_task(block, handlers: dict) -> str:
1286+ global _bg_counter
1287+ _bg_counter += 1
1288+ bg_id = f"bg_{_bg_counter:04d}"
1289+ command = block.input.get("command", block.name)
1290+
1291+ def worker():
1292+ handler = handlers.get(block.name)
1293+ result = call_tool_handler(handler, block.input, block.name)
1294+ trigger_hooks("PostToolUse", block, result)
1295+ with background_lock:
1296+ background_tasks[bg_id]["status"] = "completed"
1297+ background_results[bg_id] = str(result)
1298+
1299+ with background_lock:
1300+ background_tasks[bg_id] = {
1301+ "tool_use_id": block.id,
1302+ "command": command,
1303+ "status": "running",
1304+ }
1305+ threading.Thread(target=worker, daemon=True).start()
1306+ print(f" \033[33m[background] {bg_id}: {str(command)[:60]}\033[0m")
1307+ return bg_id
1308+
1309+
1310+def collect_background_results() -> list[str]:
1311+ with background_lock:
1312+ ready = [bg_id for bg_id, task in background_tasks.items()
1313+ if task["status"] == "completed"]
1314+ notifications = []
1315+ for bg_id in ready:
1316+ with background_lock:
1317+ task = background_tasks.pop(bg_id)
1318+ output = background_results.pop(bg_id, "")
1319+ summary = output[:200] if len(output) > 200 else output
1320+ notifications.append(
1321+ f"<task_notification>\n"
1322+ f" <task_id>{bg_id}</task_id>\n"
1323+ f" <status>completed</status>\n"
1324+ f" <command>{task['command']}</command>\n"
1325+ f" <summary>{summary}</summary>\n"
1326+ f"</task_notification>")
1327+ return notifications
1328+
1329+
1330+# ── Cron Scheduler ──
1331+
1332+# Cron jobs are stored separately from conversation history. When a job fires,
1333+# it becomes a scheduled prompt that is injected back into the same agent loop.
1334+DURABLE_PATH = WORKDIR / ".scheduled_tasks.json"
1335+
1336+
1337+@dataclass
1338+class CronJob:
1339+ id: str
1340+ cron: str
1341+ prompt: str
1342+ recurring: bool
1343+ durable: bool
1344+
1345+
1346+scheduled_jobs: dict[str, CronJob] = {}
1347+cron_queue: list[CronJob] = []
1348+cron_lock = threading.Lock()
1349+_last_fired: dict[str, str] = {}
1350+
1351+
1352+def _cron_field_matches(field: str, value: int) -> bool:
1353+ if field == "*":
1354+ return True
1355+ if field.startswith("*/"):
1356+ step = int(field[2:])
1357+ return step > 0 and value % step == 0
1358+ if "," in field:
1359+ return any(_cron_field_matches(part.strip(), value)
1360+ for part in field.split(","))
1361+ if "-" in field:
1362+ lo, hi = field.split("-", 1)
1363+ return int(lo) <= value <= int(hi)
1364+ return value == int(field)
1365+
1366+
1367+def cron_matches(cron_expr: str, dt: datetime) -> bool:
1368+ fields = cron_expr.strip().split()
1369+ if len(fields) != 5:
1370+ return False
1371+ minute, hour, dom, month, dow = fields
1372+ dow_val = (dt.weekday() + 1) % 7
1373+ m = _cron_field_matches(minute, dt.minute)
1374+ h = _cron_field_matches(hour, dt.hour)
1375+ dom_ok = _cron_field_matches(dom, dt.day)
1376+ month_ok = _cron_field_matches(month, dt.month)
1377+ dow_ok = _cron_field_matches(dow, dow_val)
1378+ if not (m and h and month_ok):
1379+ return False
1380+ if dom == "*" and dow == "*":
1381+ return True
1382+ if dom == "*":
1383+ return dow_ok
1384+ if dow == "*":
1385+ return dom_ok
1386+ return dom_ok or dow_ok
1387+
1388+
1389+def _validate_cron_field(field: str, lo: int, hi: int) -> str | None:
1390+ if field == "*":
1391+ return None
1392+ if field.startswith("*/"):
1393+ step = field[2:]
1394+ if not step.isdigit() or int(step) <= 0:
1395+ return f"Invalid step: {field}"
1396+ return None
1397+ if "," in field:
1398+ for part in field.split(","):
1399+ err = _validate_cron_field(part.strip(), lo, hi)
1400+ if err:
1401+ return err
1402+ return None
1403+ if "-" in field:
1404+ left, right = field.split("-", 1)
1405+ if not left.isdigit() or not right.isdigit():
1406+ return f"Invalid range: {field}"
1407+ a, b = int(left), int(right)
1408+ if a < lo or a > hi or b < lo or b > hi:
1409+ return f"Range {field} out of bounds [{lo}-{hi}]"
1410+ if a > b:
1411+ return f"Range start > end: {field}"
1412+ return None
1413+ if not field.isdigit():
1414+ return f"Invalid field: {field}"
1415+ value = int(field)
1416+ if value < lo or value > hi:
1417+ return f"Value {value} out of bounds [{lo}-{hi}]"
1418+ return None
1419+
1420+
1421+def validate_cron(cron_expr: str) -> str | None:
1422+ fields = cron_expr.strip().split()
1423+ if len(fields) != 5:
1424+ return f"Expected 5 fields, got {len(fields)}"
1425+ bounds = [(0, 59), (0, 23), (1, 31), (1, 12), (0, 6)]
1426+ names = ["minute", "hour", "day-of-month", "month", "day-of-week"]
1427+ for field, (lo, hi), name in zip(fields, bounds, names):
1428+ err = _validate_cron_field(field, lo, hi)
1429+ if err:
1430+ return f"{name}: {err}"
1431+ return None
1432+
1433+
1434+def save_durable_jobs():
1435+ durable = [asdict(job) for job in scheduled_jobs.values() if job.durable]
1436+ DURABLE_PATH.write_text(json.dumps(durable, indent=2))
1437+
1438+
1439+def load_durable_jobs():
1440+ if not DURABLE_PATH.exists():
1441+ return
1442+ try:
1443+ for item in json.loads(DURABLE_PATH.read_text()):
1444+ job = CronJob(**item)
1445+ if not validate_cron(job.cron):
1446+ scheduled_jobs[job.id] = job
1447+ except Exception:
1448+ pass
1449+
1450+
1451+def schedule_job(cron: str, prompt: str,
1452+ recurring: bool = True, durable: bool = True) -> CronJob | str:
1453+ err = validate_cron(cron)
1454+ if err:
1455+ return err
1456+ job = CronJob(
1457+ id=f"cron_{random.randint(0, 999999):06d}",
1458+ cron=cron, prompt=prompt,
1459+ recurring=recurring, durable=durable)
1460+ with cron_lock:
1461+ scheduled_jobs[job.id] = job
1462+ if durable:
1463+ save_durable_jobs()
1464+ return job
1465+
1466+
1467+def cancel_job(job_id: str) -> str:
1468+ with cron_lock:
1469+ job = scheduled_jobs.pop(job_id, None)
1470+ if not job:
1471+ return f"Job {job_id} not found"
1472+ if job.durable:
1473+ save_durable_jobs()
1474+ return f"Cancelled {job_id}"
1475+
1476+
1477+def cron_scheduler_loop():
1478+ while True:
1479+ time.sleep(1)
1480+ now = datetime.now()
1481+ marker = now.strftime("%Y-%m-%d %H:%M")
1482+ with cron_lock:
1483+ for job in list(scheduled_jobs.values()):
1484+ try:
1485+ if cron_matches(job.cron, now) and _last_fired.get(job.id) != marker:
1486+ cron_queue.append(job)
1487+ _last_fired[job.id] = marker
1488+ if not job.recurring:
1489+ scheduled_jobs.pop(job.id, None)
1490+ if job.durable:
1491+ save_durable_jobs()
1492+ except Exception as e:
1493+ print(f" \033[31m[cron error] {job.id}: {e}\033[0m")
1494+
1495+
1496+def consume_cron_queue() -> list[CronJob]:
1497+ with cron_lock:
1498+ fired = list(cron_queue)
1499+ cron_queue.clear()
1500+ return fired
1501+
1502+
1503+def run_schedule_cron(cron: str, prompt: str,
1504+ recurring: bool = True, durable: bool = True) -> str:
1505+ result = schedule_job(cron, prompt, recurring, durable)
1506+ if isinstance(result, str):
1507+ return f"Error: {result}"
1508+ return f"Scheduled {result.id}: '{cron}' -> {prompt}"
1509+
1510+
1511+def run_list_crons() -> str:
1512+ with cron_lock:
1513+ jobs = list(scheduled_jobs.values())
1514+ if not jobs:
1515+ return "No cron jobs."
1516+ return "\n".join(
1517+ f" {job.id}: '{job.cron}' -> {job.prompt[:40]} "
1518+ f"[{'recurring' if job.recurring else 'one-shot'}, "
1519+ f"{'durable' if job.durable else 'session'}]"
1520+ for job in jobs)
1521+
1522+
1523+def run_cancel_cron(job_id: str) -> str:
1524+ return cancel_job(job_id)
1525+
1526+
1527+load_durable_jobs()
1528+threading.Thread(target=cron_scheduler_loop, daemon=True).start()
1529+
1530+
1531+# ── MCP System ──
1532+
1533+# MCP is modeled as late-bound tools: connect first, then discovered server
1534+# tools are merged into the normal tool pool with mcp__server__tool names.
6601535class MCPClient:
6611536 """Discovers and calls tools on an MCP server (mock for teaching)."""
6621537
6631538 def __init__(self, name: str):
6641539 self.name = name
6651540 self.tools: list[dict] = []
6661541 self._handlers: dict[str, callable] = {}
6671542
6681543 def register(self, tool_defs: list[dict],
6691544 handlers: dict[str, callable]):
6701545 self.tools = tool_defs
6711546 self._handlers = handlers
6721547
6731548 def call_tool(self, tool_name: str, args: dict) -> str:
6741549 handler = self._handlers.get(tool_name)
6751550 if not handler:
6761551 return f"MCP error: unknown tool '{tool_name}'"
6771552 try:
6781553 return handler(**args)
6791554 except Exception as e:
6801555 return f"MCP error: {e}"
6811556
6821557
6831558mcp_clients: dict[str, MCPClient] = {}
6841559
6851560_DISALLOWED_CHARS = re.compile(r'[^a-zA-Z0-9_-]')
6861561
6871562
6881563def normalize_mcp_name(name: str) -> str:
6891564 """Replace non [a-zA-Z0-9_-] with underscore."""
6901565 return _DISALLOWED_CHARS.sub('_', name)
6911566
6921567
6931568def _mock_server_docs():
6941569 client = MCPClient("docs")
6951570 client.register(
6961571 tool_defs=[
6971572 {"name": "search", "description": "Search documentation. (readOnly)",
6981573 "inputSchema": {"type": "object",
6991574 "properties": {"query": {"type": "string"}},
7001575 "required": ["query"]}},
7011576 {"name": "get_version", "description": "Get API version. (readOnly)",
7021577 "inputSchema": {"type": "object", "properties": {},
7031578 "required": []}},
7041579 ],
7051580 handlers={
7061581 "search": lambda query: f"[docs] Found 3 results for '{query}'",
7071582 "get_version": lambda: "[docs] API v2.1.0",
7081583 })
7091584 return client
7101585
7111586
7121587def _mock_server_deploy():
7131588 client = MCPClient("deploy")
7141589 client.register(
7151590 tool_defs=[
7161591 {"name": "trigger",
7171592 "description": "Trigger a deployment. (destructive — requires approval in real CC)",
7181593 "inputSchema": {"type": "object",
7191594 "properties": {"service": {"type": "string"}},
7201595 "required": ["service"]}},
7211596 {"name": "status", "description": "Check deployment status. (readOnly)",
7221597 "inputSchema": {"type": "object",
7231598 "properties": {"service": {"type": "string"}},
7241599 "required": ["service"]}},
7251600 ],
7261601 handlers={
7271602 "trigger": lambda service: f"[deploy] Triggered: {service}",
7281603 "status": lambda service: f"[deploy] {service}: running (v1.4.2)",
7291604 })
7301605 return client
7311606
7321607
7331608MOCK_SERVERS = {
7341609 "docs": _mock_server_docs,
7351610 "deploy": _mock_server_deploy,
7361611}
7371612
7381613
7391614def connect_mcp(name: str) -> str:
7401615 if name in mcp_clients:
7411616 return f"MCP server '{name}' already connected"
7421617 factory = MOCK_SERVERS.get(name)
7431618 if not factory:
7441619 available = ", ".join(MOCK_SERVERS.keys())
7451620 return f"Unknown server '{name}'. Available: {available}"
7461621 mcp_client = factory()
7471622 mcp_clients[name] = mcp_client
7481623 tool_names = [t["name"] for t in mcp_client.tools]
7491624 print(f" \033[31m[mcp] connected: {name} → {tool_names}\033[0m")
7501625 return (f"Connected to MCP server '{name}'. "
7511626 f"Discovered {len(mcp_client.tools)} tools: {', '.join(tool_names)}")
7521627
7531628
7541629def assemble_tool_pool() -> tuple[list[dict], dict]:
755- """Assemble builtin tools + all MCP tools into one pool."""
1630+ """Merge builtin tools + all MCP tools into one pool."""
7561631 tools = list(BUILTIN_TOOLS)
7571632 handlers = dict(BUILTIN_HANDLERS)
7581633 for server_name, mcp_client in mcp_clients.items():
7591634 safe_server = normalize_mcp_name(server_name)
7601635 for tool_def in mcp_client.tools:
7611636 safe_tool = normalize_mcp_name(tool_def["name"])
7621637 prefixed = f"mcp__{safe_server}__{safe_tool}"
7631638 tools.append({
7641639 "name": prefixed,
7651640 "description": tool_def.get("description", ""),
7661641 "input_schema": tool_def.get("inputSchema", {}),
7671642 })
7681643 handlers[prefixed] = (
7691644 lambda *, c=mcp_client, t=tool_def["name"], **kw: c.call_tool(t, kw))
7701645 return tools, handlers
7711646
7721647
7731648# ── Lead Worktree Tools ──
7741649
7751650def run_create_worktree(name: str, task_id: str = "") -> str:
7761651 return create_worktree(name, task_id)
7771652
7781653def run_remove_worktree(name: str, discard_changes: bool = False) -> str:
7791654 return remove_worktree(name, discard_changes)
7801655
7811656def run_keep_worktree(name: str) -> str:
7821657 return keep_worktree(name)
7831658
7841659
7851660# ── Basic tool handlers ──
7861661
7871662def run_create_task(subject: str, description: str = "",
7881663 blockedBy: list[str] | None = None) -> str:
7891664 task = create_task(subject, description, blockedBy)
7901665 deps = f" (blockedBy: {', '.join(blockedBy)})" if blockedBy else ""
7911666 print(f" \033[34m[create] {task.subject}{deps}\033[0m")
7921667 return f"Created {task.id}: {task.subject}{deps}"
7931668
7941669
7951670def run_list_tasks() -> str:
7961671 tasks = list_tasks()
7971672 if not tasks:
7981673 return "No tasks."
7991674 return "\n".join(
8001675 f" {t.id}: {t.subject} [{t.status}]"
8011676 + (f" (wt:{t.worktree})" if t.worktree else "")
8021677 for t in tasks)
8031678
8041679
8051680def run_get_task(task_id: str) -> str:
806- return get_task_json(task_id)
1681+ try:
1682+ return get_task_json(task_id)
1683+ except FileNotFoundError:
1684+ return f"Error: task {task_id} not found"
8071685
8081686def run_claim_task(task_id: str) -> str:
809- return claim_task(task_id, owner="agent")
1687+ try:
1688+ return claim_task(task_id, owner="agent")
1689+ except FileNotFoundError:
1690+ return f"Error: task {task_id} not found"
8101691
8111692def run_complete_task(task_id: str) -> str:
812- return complete_task(task_id)
1693+ try:
1694+ return complete_task(task_id)
1695+ except FileNotFoundError:
1696+ return f"Error: task {task_id} not found"
8131697
8141698def run_spawn_teammate(name: str, role: str, prompt: str) -> str:
8151699 return spawn_teammate_thread(name, role, prompt)
8161700
8171701def run_send_message(to: str, content: str) -> str:
8181702 BUS.send("lead", to, content)
8191703 return f"Sent to {to}"
8201704
8211705def run_check_inbox() -> str:
8221706 msgs = consume_lead_inbox(route_protocol=True)
8231707 if not msgs:
8241708 return "(inbox empty)"
8251709 lines = []
8261710 for m in msgs:
8271711 meta = m.get("metadata", {})
8281712 req_id = meta.get("request_id", "")
8291713 tag = f" [{m['type']} req:{req_id}]" if req_id else f" [{m['type']}]"
8301714 lines.append(f" [{m['from']}]{tag} {m['content'][:200]}")
8311715 return "\n".join(lines)
8321716
8331717def run_connect_mcp(name: str) -> str:
8341718 return connect_mcp(name)
8351719
8361720
8371721# ── Tool Definitions ──
8381722
1723+# The model sees tool schemas; Python executes handlers. S20 keeps both tables
1724+# explicit so every added capability is visible in one place.
8391725BUILTIN_TOOLS = [
8401726 {"name": "bash", "description": "Run a shell command.",
8411727 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
842- "properties": {"command": {"type": "string"}},
1728+ "properties": {"command": {"type": "string"},
1729+ "run_in_background": {"type": "boolean"}},
8431730 "required": ["command"]}},
8441731 {"name": "read_file", "description": "Read file contents.",
8451732 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8461733 "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"},
847- "limit": {"type": "integer"}},
1734+ "limit": {"type": "integer"},
1735+ "offset": {"type": "integer"}},
8481736 "required": ["path"]}},
8491737 {"name": "write_file", "description": "Write content to a file.",
8501738 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8511739 "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"},
8521740 "content": {"type": "string"}},
8531741 "required": ["path", "content"]}},
1742+ {"name": "edit_file", "description": "Replace exact text in a file once.",
1743+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
1744+ "properties": {"path": {"type": "string"},
1745+ "old_text": {"type": "string"},
1746+ "new_text": {"type": "string"}},
1747+ "required": ["path", "old_text", "new_text"]}},
1748+ {"name": "glob", "description": "Find files matching a glob pattern.",
1749+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
1750+ "properties": {"pattern": {"type": "string"}},
1751+ "required": ["pattern"]}},
1752+ {"name": "todo_write",
1753+ "description": "Create and manage a task list for the current session.",
1754+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
1755+ "properties": {"todos": {"type": "array",
1756+ "items": {"type": "object",
1757+ "properties": {
1758+ "content": {"type": "string"},
1759+ "status": {"type": "string",
1760+ "enum": ["pending", "in_progress", "completed"]}},
1761+ "required": ["content", "status"]}}},
1762+ "required": ["todos"]}},
1763+ {"name": "task",
1764+ "description": "Launch a focused subagent. Returns only its final summary.",
1765+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
1766+ "properties": {"description": {"type": "string"}},
1767+ "required": ["description"]}},
1768+ {"name": "load_skill",
1769+ "description": "Load the full content of a skill by name.",
1770+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
1771+ "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"}},
1772+ "required": ["name"]}},
1773+ {"name": "compact",
1774+ "description": "Summarize earlier conversation and continue with compacted context.",
1775+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
1776+ "properties": {"focus": {"type": "string"}},
1777+ "required": []}},
8541778 {"name": "create_task", "description": "Create a task.",
8551779 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8561780 "properties": {"subject": {"type": "string"},
8571781 "description": {"type": "string"},
8581782 "blockedBy": {"type": "array",
8591783 "items": {"type": "string"}}},
8601784 "required": ["subject"]}},
8611785 {"name": "list_tasks", "description": "List all tasks.",
8621786 "input_schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {}, "required": []}},
8631787 {"name": "get_task", "description": "Get full task details.",
8641788 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8651789 "properties": {"task_id": {"type": "string"}},
8661790 "required": ["task_id"]}},
8671791 {"name": "claim_task", "description": "Claim a pending task.",
8681792 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8691793 "properties": {"task_id": {"type": "string"}},
8701794 "required": ["task_id"]}},
8711795 {"name": "complete_task", "description": "Complete an in-progress task.",
8721796 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8731797 "properties": {"task_id": {"type": "string"}},
8741798 "required": ["task_id"]}},
1799+ {"name": "schedule_cron",
1800+ "description": ("Schedule a cron job. cron is 5-field: min hour dom "
1801+ "month dow. For one-shot reminders, compute the target "
1802+ "minute and set recurring=false."),
1803+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
1804+ "properties": {"cron": {"type": "string"},
1805+ "prompt": {"type": "string"},
1806+ "recurring": {"type": "boolean"},
1807+ "durable": {"type": "boolean"}},
1808+ "required": ["cron", "prompt"]}},
1809+ {"name": "list_crons", "description": "List registered cron jobs.",
1810+ "input_schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {}, "required": []}},
1811+ {"name": "cancel_cron", "description": "Cancel a cron job by ID.",
1812+ "input_schema": {"type": "object",
1813+ "properties": {"job_id": {"type": "string"}},
1814+ "required": ["job_id"]}},
8751815 {"name": "spawn_teammate", "description": "Spawn an autonomous teammate.",
8761816 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8771817 "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"},
8781818 "role": {"type": "string"},
8791819 "prompt": {"type": "string"}},
8801820 "required": ["name", "role", "prompt"]}},
8811821 {"name": "send_message", "description": "Send message to a teammate.",
8821822 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8831823 "properties": {"to": {"type": "string"},
8841824 "content": {"type": "string"}},
8851825 "required": ["to", "content"]}},
8861826 {"name": "check_inbox",
8871827 "description": "Check inbox for messages and protocol responses.",
8881828 "input_schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {}, "required": []}},
8891829 {"name": "request_shutdown",
8901830 "description": "Request a teammate to shut down.",
8911831 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8921832 "properties": {"teammate": {"type": "string"}},
8931833 "required": ["teammate"]}},
8941834 {"name": "request_plan",
8951835 "description": "Ask a teammate to submit a plan.",
8961836 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
8971837 "properties": {"teammate": {"type": "string"},
8981838 "task": {"type": "string"}},
8991839 "required": ["teammate", "task"]}},
9001840 {"name": "review_plan",
9011841 "description": "Approve or reject a submitted plan.",
9021842 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
9031843 "properties": {"request_id": {"type": "string"},
9041844 "approve": {"type": "boolean"},
9051845 "feedback": {"type": "string"}},
9061846 "required": ["request_id", "approve"]}},
9071847 {"name": "create_worktree",
9081848 "description": "Create an isolated git worktree.",
9091849 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
9101850 "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"},
9111851 "task_id": {"type": "string"}},
9121852 "required": ["name"]}},
9131853 {"name": "remove_worktree",
9141854 "description": "Remove a worktree. Refuses if changes exist.",
9151855 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
9161856 "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"},
9171857 "discard_changes": {"type": "boolean"}},
9181858 "required": ["name"]}},
9191859 {"name": "keep_worktree",

9211861 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
9221862 "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"}},
9231863 "required": ["name"]}},
9241864 {"name": "connect_mcp",

9261866 "input_schema": {"type": "object",
9271867 "properties": {"name": {"type": "string"}},
9281868 "required": ["name"]}},
9291869]
9301870
9311871BUILTIN_HANDLERS = {
9321872 "bash": run_bash, "read_file": run_read, "write_file": run_write,

1874+ "todo_write": run_todo_write, "task": spawn_subagent,
1875+ "load_skill": load_skill,

9461892
9471893# ── Context ──
9481894
9491895MEMORY_DIR = WORKDIR / ".memory"
9501896MEMORY_INDEX = MEMORY_DIR / "MEMORY.md"
9511897
9521898
9531899def update_context(context: dict, messages: list) -> dict:
9541900 memories = ""
9551901 if MEMORY_INDEX.exists():
9561902 memories = MEMORY_INDEX.read_text()[:2000]
957- return {"memories": memories}
1903+ return {
1904+ "memories": memories,
1905+ "connected_mcp": list(mcp_clients.keys()),
1906+ "active_teammates": list(active_teammates.keys()),
1907+ }
9581908
9591909
960-# ── Agent Loop (s19: dynamic tool pool, no prompt cache) ──
1910+# ── Agent Loop ──
9611911
1912+rounds_since_todo = 0
1913+agent_lock = threading.Lock()
1914+
1915+
1916+def prepare_context(messages: list) -> list:
1917+ # Every LLM turn enters through the same context budget pipeline.
1918+ messages[:] = tool_result_budget(messages)
1919+ messages[:] = snip_compact(messages)
1920+ messages[:] = micro_compact(messages)
1921+ if estimate_size(messages) > CONTEXT_LIMIT:
1922+ messages[:] = compact_history(messages)
1923+ return messages
1924+
1925+
1926+def build_user_content(results: list[dict]) -> list[dict]:
1927+ # Tool results and completed background notifications are both returned to
1928+ # the model as user-side content, matching the tool_result feedback loop.
1929+ content = list(results)
1930+ for note in collect_background_results():
1931+ content.append({"type": "text", "text": note})
1932+ return content
1933+
1934+
1935+def inject_background_notifications(messages: list):
1936+ notes = collect_background_results()
1937+ if notes:
1938+ messages.append({"role": "user", "content": [
1939+ {"type": "text", "text": note} for note in notes]})
1940+
1941+
1942+def call_llm(messages: list, context: dict, tools: list,
1943+ state: RecoveryState, max_tokens: int):
1944+ system = assemble_system_prompt(context)
1945+ return with_retry(
1946+ lambda: client.messages.create(
1947+ model=state.current_model,
1948+ system=system,
1949+ messages=messages,
1950+ tools=tools,
1951+ max_tokens=max_tokens),
1952+ state)
1953+
1954+
9621955def agent_loop(messages: list, context: dict):
1956+ global rounds_since_todo
9631957 tools, handlers = assemble_tool_pool()
964- system = assemble_system_prompt(context)
1958+ state = RecoveryState()
1959+ max_tokens = DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS
1960+
9651961 while True:
1962+ # One cycle: inject scheduled/background work, prepare context, call
1963+ # the model, execute tool_use blocks, append tool_results, repeat.
1964+ fired = consume_cron_queue()
1965+ for job in fired:
1966+ messages.append({"role": "user",
1967+ "content": f"[Scheduled] {job.prompt}"})
1968+ print(f" \033[35m[cron inject] {job.prompt[:60]}\033[0m")
1969+
1970+ inject_background_notifications(messages)
1971+
1972+ if rounds_since_todo >= 3:
1973+ messages.append({"role": "user",
1974+ "content": "<reminder>Update your todos.</reminder>"})
1975+ rounds_since_todo = 0
1976+
1977+ prepare_context(messages)
1978+ context = update_context(context, messages)
1979+ tools, handlers = assemble_tool_pool()
1980+
9661981 try:
967- response = client.messages.create(
968- model=MODEL, system=system, messages=messages,
969- tools=tools, max_tokens=8000)
1982+ response = call_llm(messages, context, tools, state, max_tokens)
9701983 except Exception as e:
1984+ if is_prompt_too_long_error(e) and not state.has_attempted_reactive_compact:
1985+ messages[:] = reactive_compact(messages)
1986+ state.has_attempted_reactive_compact = True
1987+ continue
9711988 messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": [
9721989 {"type": "text", "text": f"[Error] {type(e).__name__}: {e}"}]})
9731990 return
9741991
1992+ if response.stop_reason == "max_tokens":
1993+ if not state.has_escalated:
1994+ max_tokens = ESCALATED_MAX_TOKENS
1995+ state.has_escalated = True
1996+ print(f" \033[33m[max_tokens] retry with {max_tokens}\033[0m")
1997+ continue
1998+ messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response.content})
1999+ if state.recovery_count < MAX_RECOVERY_RETRIES:
2000+ messages.append({"role": "user", "content": CONTINUATION_PROMPT})
2001+ state.recovery_count += 1
2002+ continue
2003+ return
2004+
2005+ max_tokens = DEFAULT_MAX_TOKENS
2006+ state.has_escalated = False
9752007 messages.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response.content})
976- if response.stop_reason != "tool_use":
2008+ if not has_tool_use(response.content):
2009+ trigger_hooks("Stop", messages)
9772010 return
9782011
9792012 results = []
2013+ compacted_now = False
9802014 for block in response.content:
9812015 if block.type != "tool_use":
9822016 continue
9832017 print(f"\033[36m> {block.name}\033[0m")
2018+
2019+ if block.name == "compact":
2020+ messages[:] = compact_history(messages)
2021+ messages.append({"role": "user",
2022+ "content": "[Compacted. Continue with summarized context.]"})
2023+ compacted_now = True
2024+ break
2025+
2026+ blocked = trigger_hooks("PreToolUse", block)
2027+ if blocked:
2028+ results.append({"type": "tool_result",
2029+ "tool_use_id": block.id,
2030+ "content": str(blocked)})
2031+ continue
2032+
2033+ if should_run_background(block.name, block.input):
2034+ bg_id = start_background_task(block, handlers)
2035+ output = (f"[Background task {bg_id} started] "
2036+ "Result will arrive as a task_notification.")
2037+ results.append({"type": "tool_result",
2038+ "tool_use_id": block.id,
2039+ "content": output})
2040+ continue
2041+
9842042 handler = handlers.get(block.name)
985- output = handler(**block.input) if handler else "Unknown"
2043+ output = call_tool_handler(handler, block.input, block.name)
2044+ trigger_hooks("PostToolUse", block, output)
9862045 print(str(output)[:300])
2046+
2047+ if block.name == "todo_write":
2048+ rounds_since_todo = 0
2049+ else:
2050+ rounds_since_todo += 1
2051+
9872052 results.append({"type": "tool_result",
9882053 "tool_use_id": block.id, "content": output})
989- messages.append({"role": "user", "content": results})
9902054
991- if any(b.name == "connect_mcp" for b in response.content
992- if b.type == "tool_use"):
993- tools, handlers = assemble_tool_pool()
994- context = update_context(context, messages)
995- system = assemble_system_prompt(context)
2055+ if compacted_now:
2056+ continue
9962057
2058+ messages.append({"role": "user", "content": build_user_content(results)})
9972059
2060+
2061+def print_turn_assistants(messages: list, turn_start: int):
2062+ for msg in messages[turn_start:]:
2063+ if msg.get("role") != "assistant":
2064+ continue
2065+ for block in msg.get("content", []):
2066+ if block_type(block) == "text":
2067+ terminal_print(block["text"] if isinstance(block, dict) else block.text)
2068+
2069+
2070+def cron_autorun_loop(history: list, context: dict):
2071+ while True:
2072+ time.sleep(1)
2073+ fired = consume_cron_queue()
2074+ if not fired:
2075+ continue
2076+ with agent_lock:
2077+ turn_start = len(history)
2078+ for job in fired:
2079+ history.append({"role": "user",
2080+ "content": f"[Scheduled] {job.prompt}"})
2081+ terminal_print(
2082+ f" \033[35m[cron auto] {job.prompt[:60]}\033[0m")
2083+ agent_loop(history, context)
2084+ context.update(update_context(context, history))
2085+ print_turn_assistants(history, turn_start)
2086+
2087+
9982088if __name__ == "__main__":
999- print("s19: mcp tools")
2089+ CLI_ACTIVE = True
2090+ print("s20: comprehensive agent")
10002091 print("Enter a question, press Enter to send. Type q to quit.\n")
10012092 history = []
1002- context = {"memories": ""}
2093+ context = update_context({}, [])
2094+ threading.Thread(target=cron_autorun_loop,
2095+ args=(history, context), daemon=True).start()
10032096 while True:
10042097 try:
1005- query = input("\033[36ms19 >> \033[0m")
2098+ query = input(PROMPT)
10062099 except (EOFError, KeyboardInterrupt):
10072100 break
10082101 if query.strip().lower() in ("q", "exit", ""):
10092102 break
2103+ trigger_hooks("UserPromptSubmit", query)
2104+ turn_start = len(history)
10102105 history.append({"role": "user", "content": query})
1011- agent_loop(history, context)
1012- context = update_context(context, history)
1013- for block in history[-1]["content"]:
1014- if getattr(block, "type", None) == "text":
1015- print(block.text)
1016- elif isinstance(block, dict) and block.get("type") == "text":
1017- print(block.get("text", ""))
2106+ with agent_lock:
2107+ agent_loop(history, context)
2108+ context = update_context(context, history)
2109+ print_turn_assistants(history, turn_start)
10182110
10192111 inbox = consume_lead_inbox(route_protocol=True)
10202112 if inbox:
2113+ def inbox_label(msg):
2114+ req_id = msg.get("metadata", {}).get("request_id", "")
2115+ suffix = f" req:{req_id}" if req_id else ""
2116+ return f"{msg.get('type', 'message')}{suffix}"
2117+
10212118 inbox_text = "\n".join(
1022- f"From {m['from']} [{m.get('type', 'message')}]: "
2119+ f"From {m['from']} [{inbox_label(m)}]: "
10232120 f"{m['content'][:200]}" for m in inbox)
10242121 history.append({"role": "user",
10252122 "content": f"[Inbox]\n{inbox_text}"})
10262123 print()
